rulings.Case Title: JACKIE GEORGE VS. NORMA BRANDON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moton Of Plaintiff For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 2, 2013, line 1. PLAINTIFF JACKIE GEORGE's Moton For Sanctions GRANTED. Plaintiff's motion for terminating sanctions and grant request for monetary sanctions in the amount of $497.50. = (501/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: 1369 HYDE STREET, LTD VS. WILLIAM B. REILLY et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 2, 2013, line 2. CROSS DEFENDANT 1369 HYDE STREET, LTD's MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT Granted with leave to amend to allege facts in support of punitive damages against business entity; granted without leave to amend as to "retaliatory eviction" and "spoliation" allegations, opposition fails to address their relevance and materiality to alleged causes of action. = (501/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: 1369 HYDE STREET, LTD VS. WILLIAM B. REILLY et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 2, 2013, line 2. CROSS DEFENDANT 1369 HYDE STREET, LTD's AMENDED DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT Sustained with leave to amend to allege breached terms of the contract written/oral or implied and allege facts in support of those breaches. = (501/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PAYMON GHAFOURI et al VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A., A BUSINESS ENTITY et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 2, 2013, line 3. DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Continued to May 14, 2013. Department and time will be announced by tentative ruling on May 13, 2013. = (501/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: HENRY SLMAOVICH VS. MIKHAIL EKCHENGUER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 2, 2013, line 4. DEFENDANT EKSHENGER, ILIA SUED AS ILIYA EKCHENGUER's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND DENIED. Moving party failed to carry its burden to show excusable neglect and failed to attach proposed responsive pleadings to his motion. = (501/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: N.R. PATEL DBA HENRY HOTEL et al VS. CHARLES JOHNSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions As To Pltf For Filing A Frivilous Motion; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 2, 2013, line 5. DEFENDANT CHARLES JOHNSON's Motion For Sanctions As To Pltf For Filing A Frivilous Motion; Request For Judicial Notice DENIED. CCP 1008 provides in relevant part: "A violation of this section may be punished as a contempt and with sanctions as allowed by Section 128.7." Moving party fails to address compliance with CCP 128.7(c). = (501/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: QUINCY YU et al VS. JEFFREY YU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion By Pltf For A Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 2, 2013, line 6. PLAINTIFF QUINCY YU and KAY YU's Motion For A Protective Order GRANTED as to Special Interrogatories. Propounding party's declaration fails to show how 356 special interrogatories to each plaintiff are warranted at this stage of litigation for unlawful detainer based on non-payment of rent. Protective order as to deposition of third party is DENIED. No supporting evidence is provided. Sanctions granted in the amount of $750. = (501/EHG)
